opp.ais_why:
• Oct 2025 Avoma notes capture Refresco CPO Coert Michielsen articulating that 'AI and data lakes could overcome integration barriers' across 35 acquired ERPs ‚Äî a verbatim buyer-side framing of AI as the integration-solution layer. Plus an explicit Zycus action item to 'send additional information regarding the elements of Gen AI in Zycus, including details on the autonomous negotiation agent and contract management features.' Plan includes deploying Zycus intake manager via MS Teams.
• Qualified stage, BD Engaged; CFO-level discussion of EUR 3.5B addressable spend with 70% direct-spend focus; SRM and Merlin Intake aligned to indirect spend control. Caroline Lacocque + Hemendra Chaturvedi + Varun Kukreja engaged with executive-level buyer (Coert Michielsen ‚Äî CPO). Demo scheduled. Last activity Mar 30, 2026.
• High confidence based on rich Avoma evidence with CPO-spoken AI framing tied to a concrete pain (35 ERP integrations post-acquisition). Held below 9-10 because no RFP or POC is committed yet and customer also voiced 'do we really have to implement the complete software' objection ‚Äî some scope skepticism counterbalances the AI enthusiasm. Score 8 reflects buyer-spoken AI-as-integration-solution + autonomous-negotiation interest.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 30 Oct 2025: discovery call notes with Coert

Coert Michielsen stated on 30 Oct 2025 discovery call: Goal to harmonize ERP systems and automate processes to reduce manual headcount across regions, increase PR-to-PO adoption using Zycus intake agent to raise efficiency from 70% to 95%, focus initially on direct spend (€3.5B total, ~70% direct) and improving Europe's decentralized operations
metrics.narrative: Business problem is clearly articulated: Refresco's 35-acquisition history created ERP fragmentation (many systems) that forces manual processes, excessive back-office headcount, and low PR-to-PO adoption (currently 70%, target 95%). Coert stated on 30 Oct 2025 discovery call that the goal is to harmonize ERP systems, automate processes to reduce manual headcount across regions, and raise PR-to-PO efficiency from 70% to 95% using the Zycus intake agent. Total spend scope is €3.5B with ~70% direct spend, focus initially on direct spend and Europe's decentralized operations. Pain is confirmed and quantified (PR-to-PO gap, headcount reduction goal, €3.5B spend base). However, no financial impact quantified (cost per manual process, FTE reduction target, projected savings/ROI, payback period). The promised ROI assessment with procurement controller and European director (stated next step 30 Oct 2025) never occurred, so quantified value case remains incomplete. Gap: financial ROI not yet built with the buyer.

champion.narrative: Champion is weak and at high risk. Coert Michielsen (Group Director Purchasing) was the sole contact on the 30 Oct 2025 discovery call and appeared engaged at that time, stating clear next steps (invite procurement controller and European director for ROI assessment and demo). However, Coert has been unresponsive for 88 days (last activity 30 Mar 2026) and has not delivered on the stated next step for 239 days. No OpportunityContactRole for Champion is logged. No evidence of advocacy: Coert has not introduced other stakeholders, has not driven internal momentum, has not pushed back on competitors (no competitive intel exists), and has not responded to Zycus outreach. Access to Economic Buyer or Decision Maker is unknown; Jan Veldhuis (CFO, referenced in prior memory as final approver) has never been engaged, and no evidence exists that Coert can or will facilitate that connection. Current engagement: passive/cooling, effectively dormant. The deal is single-threaded on a contact who has gone silent. Gap: no active champion, no multi-threading, no advocacy evidence, and no path to the Economic Buyer.

For a Food & Beverage company of Refresco's scale (€3.5B spend, multi-ERP environment, seeking AI-driven intake and contract automation), typical competitive set includes SAP Ariba (incumbent advantage if SAP ERP is in the stack), Coupa, Ivalua, and potentially Jaggaer or GEP. The long silence (88 days), the unfulfilled multi-threading step, and the 7-month close-date slip with no explanation suggest Refresco may be quietly evaluating alternatives or the deal has stalled due to internal prioritization or budget reallocation, but Zycus has zero visibility. identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly identified and confirmed by Coert Michielsen on the 30 Oct 2025 discovery call. Operational pain: Refresco was built from 35 acquisitions, resulting in many ERP systems that delay automation and force manual processes. Europe's decentralized landscape leads to excessive back-office roles performing non-value tasks. Contract database (Ledges Way), DocuSign workflows, and e-auction tool are not integrated, hampering end-to-end automation. Financial pain: current PR-to-PO adoption is only 70%, and the lack of automation inflates headcount costs. Coert stated the excuse of ERP diversity is hindering automation and that the goal is to use AI and data lakes to bridge units and reduce manual headcount. Pain owner: Coert Michielsen, Group Director Purchasing, who owns procurement operations across regions and is accountable for the efficiency and headcount gap. Pain is confirmed and specific, tied to the multi-ERP legacy, decentralized Europe operations, and low PR-to-PO discipline.
